title: 30o: test templates omit {type}; bug not covered
description:
Location: crates/jmap-chat/tests/blob_test.rs, all download_blob tests (lines 138-238).

What is wrong: All four download_blob test cases use a URL template without the {type} variable: '{base}/download/{accountId}/{blobId}/{name}'. Because {type} is absent from the test template, the missing substitution bug (see companion issue 30o.19) is not caught by the test suite. Per RFC 8620 §6.2 the download URL MUST contain {accountId}, {blobId}, {type}, and {name}. Tests that use a non-conforming template cannot detect failures that only appear with a spec-conforming template.

Why it matters: The test oracle is insufficient — it passes silently even though the production code would fail against a real JMAP server. This is a test-coverage gap that allows the RFC 8620 §6.2 violation to ship undetected.

Fix required: Update the download test template to include {type} (e.g. '{base}/download/{accountId}/{blobId}/{name}?accept={type}') once download_blob gains the content_type parameter. Verify the wiremock path matcher is updated to match the full expanded path including the accept query parameter.
close_reason: fixed: added test 7 (download_blob_with_type_substitution) using accept_type=Some; unit tests already cover percent-encoding of slashes
